models.runSummary: A single execution of the full test suite against the running app. Tracks the build agent used, pass/fail totals, duration, and overall status.
models.epic: A top-level grouping of test results mirroring the coverage map. Contains sections, which contain stories and test cases.
models.section: A sub-grouping within an epic. Corresponds to a specific screen or area of the app under test.
models.story: A user story with one or more test cases. Has aggregate pass/fail counts from its test cases.
models.testCase: A single executable test scenario (one Gherkin scenario). The leaf node of the result tree. Carries the actual vs expected comparison, thin-assertion flag, and fix prompt.
models.thinAssertionFlag: A test case flagged as potentially hardcoded or trivially passing — no real oracle fixture behind the assertion.
models.coverageGap: A section in the manifest that has no passing behavioral test case in the latest run.
models.agentComparison: A single test case's results across multiple build agents, used for the Compare Agents tab.

relationships: A testCase may have a fixPrompt (string, oracle-scrubbed)
A testCase may have a screenshotUrl
A runSummary references many testCases via the run ID
An agentComparison references one testCase and many runSummaries (one per agent)

stories.title: Charter is generated from Design OS test.md

testCases.title: Charter includes scope, risk priorities, and correctness definition
testCases.gherkin:
Given the user has completed /test-charter
When the charter.md file is read
Then it contains a scope section
And it contains a risk priorities table
And it defines what 'correct' means for the application

testCases.title: Empty Design OS test.md produces a warning, not a crash
testCases.gherkin:
Given the Design OS test.md is empty
When /test-charter is run
Then a warning message is shown
And the user is prompted to provide at least minimal scope information

stories.title: Oracle fixtures are stored outside the app repo

testCases.title: Oracle JSON files are written to test-os/oracles/, not app repo
testCases.gherkin:
Given oracle data has been confirmed
When the filesystem is inspected
Then oracle JSON files exist under test-os/oracles/
And no oracle files exist anywhere inside the app repository

stories.title: Hermes config reads base URL from hermes-config.json

testCases.title: Hermes resolves the app base URL before running tests
testCases.gherkin:
Given hermes-config.json has a valid appUrl
When Hermes initializes
Then the base URL is resolved and the app is reachable

testCases.title: Navigation timeout config is respected
testCases.gherkin:
Given hermes-config.json sets navigation timeout to 30000ms
When a page navigation exceeds the timeout
Then the test fails with a timeout error
And the error is recorded in the results JSON
testCases.errorMessage: Expected error type 'NavigationTimeout' but received 'GenericError'. The timeout is firing but not being classified correctly in results.json.
testCases.fixPrompt:
Test 'Navigation timeout config is respected' is failing.

The test expects that when a navigation exceeds the configured timeout, the error recorded in results.json has errorType 'NavigationTimeout'. The current implementation records all timeout errors as 'GenericError'.

Behavior to fix:
- The Hermes executor should classify errors that exceed the navigation timeout specifically as NavigationTimeout rather than a generic error category
- Check the error classification logic in the run output handler
- The timeout value itself (30000ms) is being applied correctly — only the classification is wrong

Do not reference test-os/ fixture files. Fix based on the Design OS spec and Agent OS task definitions only.

stories.title: Gherkin is generated from suite scope definition

testCases.title: Generated Gherkin contains all behaviors from scope
testCases.gherkin:
Given a suite scope has been confirmed
When /author-tests generates Gherkin
Then each behavior in the scope has a corresponding Scenario
And adversarial behaviors are tagged @adversarial
And edge cases are tagged @edge-case

stories.title: Mark suite complete is guarded against unfinished steps

testCases.title: Mark Complete is disabled when capture-evidence step is not_started
testCases.gherkin:
Given a suite has author-tests complete but capture-evidence not_started
When the user opens the actions menu
Then the Mark Complete option is disabled
And a tooltip explains that all 4 steps must be complete

testCases.title: Mark Complete succeeds when all 4 steps are complete
testCases.gherkin:
Given all 4 accordion steps show status 'complete'
When the user clicks Mark Complete
Then the suite status updates to 'complete'
And a completion timestamp is recorded
And the next in-progress suite is auto-selected
testCases.errorMessage: Suite is marked complete but the next in-progress suite is not auto-selected. The activeId state is not being advanced after the completion mutation.
testCases.fixPrompt:
Test 'Mark Complete succeeds when all 4 steps are complete' is failing.

The suite is being marked complete correctly and the completion timestamp is recorded. However, after marking a suite complete, the UI does not automatically advance to the next in-progress suite in the left panel.

Behavior to fix:
- After onMarkComplete() resolves, find the next suite with status 'in_progress' (or 'not_started' if none remain in_progress) and call onSelectSuite() with that suite's ID
- If no other suites remain, show the phase-complete banner instead
- The current implementation updates the suite status but does not update the activeSuiteId

Do not reference test-os/ fixture files. Fix based on the Design OS spec and Agent OS task definitions only.

stories.title: Suite reset clears all step completions

testCases.title: Reset requires confirmation before wiping step completions
testCases.gherkin:
Given the user clicks Reset from the actions menu
When the confirmation dialog appears
And the user cancels
Then the suite is unchanged

testCases.title: Confirmed reset reverts all steps to not_started
testCases.gherkin:
Given the user confirms the reset action
When the reset completes
Then all 4 steps revert to 'not_started'
And the suite status reverts to 'not_started'
And all authored stories are cleared from the suite
testCases.errorMessage: Steps and suite status are correctly reset but authored stories are not cleared from the data layer. The stories array for the suite still contains the previously authored test cases.
testCases.fixPrompt:
Test 'Confirmed reset reverts all steps to not_started' is failing.

The suite status and step statuses are correctly reset to 'not_started'. However, the authored stories for the suite are not cleared when a reset is performed.

Behavior to fix:
- When onResetSuite() is called, the stories associated with that suiteId should also be removed from the stories collection
- The current implementation only resets the step status objects — it does not clear the authored content
- After reset, the author-tests accordion body should show the empty state (no stories)

Do not reference test-os/ fixture files. Fix based on the Design OS spec and Agent OS task definitions only.

stories.title: Screenshot gallery shows up to 5 slots per story

testCases.title: Gallery renders exactly 5 slots for each captureScreenshot story
testCases.gherkin:
Given a high-priority story has captureScreenshot set to true
When the capture-evidence accordion step is expanded
Then exactly 5 filmstrip slots are shown for that story
And captured slots show a thumbnail placeholder and step description
And uncaptured slots show a dashed outline with the step index

testCases.title: Counter updates when a new screenshot is captured
testCases.gherkin:
Given a story has 3 screenshots captured out of 5
When a new screenshot is captured for step 4
Then the counter updates from '3/5' to '4/5'
And the newly captured slot transitions from dashed to filled
testCases.errorMessage: The counter does not update reactively when a new screenshot is added. The screenshots prop is not being re-evaluated after a capture event — the component is showing stale data.
testCases.fixPrompt:
Test 'Counter updates when a new screenshot is captured' is failing.

The screenshot gallery counter (X/5) is not updating when a new screenshot is captured. The slot also does not transition from dashed to filled.

Behavior to fix:
- The capture-evidence accordion body derives screenshot counts from the screenshots prop — ensure the parent component triggers a re-render with updated screenshot data after a capture event fires
- The component may be memoizing stale screenshot counts; ensure the filmstrip re-derives from props, not from local state initialized at mount time

Do not reference test-os/ fixture files. Fix based on the Design OS spec and Agent OS task definitions only.

stories.title: Export report opens report.html in a new tab

testCases.title: Export Report opens a new browser tab pointing to report.html
testCases.gherkin:
Given the verdict dashboard is loaded
When the user clicks Export Report
Then a new browser tab opens
And it loads the static report.html file from the workspace root
testCases.errorMessage: Clicking Export Report does nothing. The button has no href or onClick handler wired up. The onExportReport callback is not being called.
testCases.fixPrompt:
Test 'Export Report opens a new browser tab pointing to report.html' is failing.

The Export Report button is rendered but clicking it does not open a new tab or call any handler.

Behavior to fix:
- The Export Report button should call onExportReport() when clicked, or alternatively render as an anchor tag with href='/report.html' and target='_blank'
- Verify the button has the correct event handler attached in the verdict dashboard component

Do not reference test-os/ fixture files. Fix based on the Design OS spec and Agent OS task definitions only.

stories.title: Emit Fix Prompt copies scrubbed prompt to clipboard

testCases.title: Copied prompt does not contain oracle values
testCases.gherkin:
Given a failed test case has a fixPrompt
When the user clicks Emit Fix Prompt
Then the prompt is copied to the clipboard
And the prompt text does not contain any oracle fixture values
And a 'Copied!' toast appears for 2 seconds

testCases.title: Thin-assertion flag is displayed on flagged test case rows
testCases.gherkin:
Given a test case has thinAssertionFlag set to true
When the result tree is rendered
Then the test case row shows a thin-assertion indicator
And the thin-assertion flags section lists this test case
testCases.errorMessage: The test case is correctly listed in the thin-assertion flags section but the inline row indicator is not rendered on the test case row in the result tree.
testCases.fixPrompt:
Test 'Thin-assertion flag is displayed on flagged test case rows' is failing.

Test cases with thinAssertionFlag=true appear in the thin-assertions section but do not show an inline indicator on their row in the result tree.

Behavior to fix:
- The test case row component should check the thinAssertionFlag field and render an amber indicator (icon or badge) when true
- The indicator should appear alongside the pass/fail status icon on the row

Do not reference test-os/ fixture files. Fix based on the Design OS spec and Agent OS task definitions only.

stories.title: Nightly cron job runs at 02:00 UTC

testCases.title: Cron entry is set to 0 2 * * * in crontab
testCases.gherkin:
Given the cron schedule has been configured
When the crontab is inspected
Then the run-tests.sh entry is scheduled for 0 2 * * *

stories.title: Run results are committed back to the Test OS repo

testCases.title: results.json and report.html are committed after each run
testCases.gherkin:
Given a test run has completed
When run-tests.sh finishes
Then runs/<run-id>/results.json is committed to the Test OS repo
And report.html is committed to the repo root
And the commit message includes the run timestamp

thinAssertions.testCaseTitle: Counter updates when a new screenshot is captured
thinAssertions.reason: Assertion checks UI counter display only; no underlying data fixture verifies the capture event was stored. Test could pass against a hardcoded counter label.

thinAssertions.testCaseTitle: Thin-assertion flag is displayed on flagged test case rows
thinAssertions.storyTitle: Emit Fix Prompt copies scrubbed prompt to clipboard
thinAssertions.reason: Expected value 'rowIndicatorVisible: true' is asserted via DOM presence check only; no oracle fixture ties the indicator to the actual thinAssertionFlag data field.

thinAssertions.testCaseTitle: Cron entry is set to 0 2 * * * in crontab
thinAssertions.storyTitle: Nightly cron job runs at 02:00 UTC
thinAssertions.reason: Test asserts cron expression by string match against a static constant. No fixture validates that the cron actually runs — only that the string is present.

coverageGaps.reason: No behavioral test cases authored for coverage map generation flow. Only structural tests exist.
Infrastructure setup section — no behavioral tests authored yet. Deferred to v0.5.
